reference: Okay, I have heard that some molds are safe to eat and kind of doubted this answer would be a yes, but I got a coconut from Whole Foods today, and it had what looked like a small portion of bluish-black, pink, and yellow mold in a small ring around where a cut area was placed for easy opening. I already threw out the milk but was wondering if I could salvage the rest of the coconut or if I shouldn't because I am allergic to mold.
text: Okay, I have heard that molds are safe to eat and kind of doubted this answer would be a yes, but I got a coconut from Whole Foods today and it had what looked like a small portion of bluish black, pink, and yellow mold in a small ring around where a cut area was placed for easy opening. I already threw out the milk but was wondering if I could salvage the rest of the coconut or if I shouldn't because I am allergic to mold.
